titlestmt.author: compiled by Jane Meditz, with the assistance of Richard Boursey and Anne Rhodes

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 30, 2025 at 20:25

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts and printed versions of original works and arrangements by Frink, theatrical scripts, clippings, ephemera, and sound recordings.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized in seven series: I. Music. II. News Clippings and Posters, III. Concert Programs and Ephemera, IV. Audiovisual Materials, V. Correspondence, IV. Obituary and Autobiography, VII. Photographs

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 76, Charles Frink Papers in the Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Charles Frink was a composer, pianist, playwrite, educator, and Yale alumnus.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Majority of musical compositions are holographs or facsimiles of holographs. Many contain later revisions. Typed or handwritten lyrics are often attached to vocal works. Musical and theatrical works regularly include introductions to the work and notes for performance.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph, pencil. Piano reduction. Originally titled "Symphony No. 3."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph, pencil. Piano reduction. Originally titled "Symphony No. 5." Musical content appears to differ from "Symphonic Suite" from 1959.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph, pencil. Piano reduction. Originally titled "Symphony No. 6."

title.part: Anthem for a Distant Place (Choir + Piano)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The last piece Frink wrote before his death.

did.unittitle: East Wind - Three Poems for Voice & Orchestra

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph, pencil. Piano reduction with 1975 revisions inserted.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph, pencil. Piano reduction / early draft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Instrumental parts. Brass, timpani, and cymbal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph, pencil. Piano reduction / early draft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph, pencil. Full score. Includes typed notes from "Solveig," conductor of the orchestra which performed the work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph, pencil. Early drafts / piano reduction.

did.unittitle: Three Songs for Mezzo-Soprano and Orchestra

title.part: When Lilacs Last in the Dooryard Bloomed (Choir + Piano)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph, pencil. Early draft / piano reduction.

did.unittitle: Another Way to Learn to Play the Piano Volume I - Introductory

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two sets of songs, "Five Songs" and additional songs designated as "companions" by Frink.

did.unittitle: Preliminary Sequence of Larger pieces of music: begun 1/27/14

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes later editions of pieces originally composed several decades earlier.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Compiled by Frink shortly before his death. Includes songs found in larger works, such as "Hands."

title.part: Standalone Songs (my own category- green folder) (mid-80's)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series contains Frink's arrangements of songs of indigenous peoples.
